fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to delaware, speech-language pathologist compact delaware, delaware speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech/Language Pathologists, Audiologists and Hearing Aid Dispensers
board.mailing_address: Cannon Building, 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904
board.physical_address: 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Delaware.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Create a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Register for a new account or log in to an existing one on the DELPROS online portal.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Submit the Speech-Language Pathologist license application through your DELPROS account and pay the required application and initial license fees.

steps.title: Submit Academic Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official graduate degree transcripts to be sent directly from your university to the Delaware Board of Speech Pathologists, Audiologists and Hearing Aid Dispensers.

steps.title: Submit Clinical Fellowship/ASHA CCCs
steps.description: Provide documentation of your supervised clinical fellowship (e.g., ASHA Certificate of Clinical Competence or a completed Verification of Clinical Fellowship Form) directly to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Praxis Scores
steps.description: Arrange for official Praxis Series exam scores (Speech-Language Pathology, #5331) to be sent directly to the Board from ETS.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Delaware permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Delaware issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Delaware is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Delaware through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $141. Biennial renewal is $111.

faqs.question: Is Delaware part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Delaware has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Delaware allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Delaware permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Delaware participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Delaware without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Delaware is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Delaware through a compact privilege.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Delaware
hero.dek: To apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist license in Delaware, users must register and complete their application through the DELPROS online portal.

official_links.registration_steps: On the DELPROS portal, click 'Register a New Account' if you don't have one, or 'Login to an Existing Account'. Follow the prompts to create your account and verify your email. Once logged in, navigate to 'Apply for a License' from your dashboard.
